steps.expected: Empty state test for runtime buffer passes
steps.observed: PASS: "shows empty state when the runtime buffer is empty"

assertions.title: Log populates as runtime events arrive

steps.expected: Non-empty runtime event log renders rows and increments count
steps.observed: PASS: "renders entries newest-first with HH:MM:SS timestamps and badges" confirms 3 rows and "3 events" count

assertions.title: Timestamp format matches Paper

steps.expected: Verify HH:MM:SS regex and monospace computed font-family on every row
steps.observed: Suite validates HH:MM:SS regex but contains no computed-style assertion for monospace font-family

assertions.issues: Assigned command evidence is insufficient to confirm the computed font-family requirement.

steps.expected: Rows should render in newest-first order
steps.observed: PASS: newest-first assertion validates badges order ["PING", "ECDH", "SIGN"] for descending timestamps

steps.expected: Expand control renders 2-space-indented multiline JSON payload
steps.observed: PASS: "expands a row to render 2-space-indented JSON of the payload" with newline + two-space indentation checks

assertions.title: Keyboard can expand / collapse rows

steps.expected: Verify Tab reachability, Enter/Space toggle behavior, aria-expanded updates, and focus return
steps.observed: No keyboard interaction test (Tab/Enter/Space/focus ring) is present in the executed suite; only mouse click expansion is covered

assertions.issues: Assigned command evidence is insufficient to confirm keyboard accessibility behavior for row expand/collapse.

blockers.description: Assigned vitest command provides no direct assertion coverage for keyboard expand/collapse interactions and no computed-style check for timestamp font family.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed all 9 executed tests in EventLogPanel.runtime.test.tsx to confirm whether those checks were present; they were not.

summary: Executed the assigned vitest command successfully (exit code 0; 1 file, 9 tests passed). Based on command evidence, VAL-EVENTLOG-001, 002, 009, and 011 pass; VAL-EVENTLOG-008 and VAL-EVENTLOG-022 are blocked due missing required coverage in the executed test suite.
